decisions.title: Project Language & Framework Choice
decisions.decision: Python 3.11+ with Flask for REST API
decisions.rationale: Python enables rapid prototyping of distributed logic. Flask is lightweight for microservices. Easier for student presentation.

decisions.title: Package Manager
decisions.decision: UV for Python dependency & environment management
decisions.rationale: Modern, fast replacement for pip/venv. Single pyproject.toml. Better UX.

decisions.title: Consistency Model
decisions.decision: Eventual Consistency with AP from CAP theorem
decisions.rationale: Matches Dynamo design. Prioritizes availability and partition tolerance. Nodes stay available even during network splits.

decisions.title: Causality Tracking Mechanism
decisions.decision: Vector Clocks (Fidge/Mattern 1988) instead of Lamport clocks
decisions.rationale: Reports exact concurrency, not just total ordering. Needed for conflict detection. Core to Dynamo design.

decisions.title: Local Storage Backend
decisions.decision: SQLite for core implementation, JSON fallback for testing
decisions.rationale: SQLite: persistent, transactional, no external service needed. JSON: simpler for demo scenarios.

decisions.title: Multi-Version Storage (Sibling Conflicts)
decisions.decision: Store all concurrent versions as siblings, return all on GET during conflict
decisions.rationale: Matches Dynamo. Client chooses resolution or system auto-merges via configurable strategy. Preserves causality.

decisions.title: Replication Model
decisions.decision: Probabilistic quorum-based peer synchronization (flexible, eventual consistency)
decisions.rationale: No fixed quorum size. Nodes sync with any available peers. Improves availability. Eventual convergence guaranteed.

decisions.title: Project Structure
decisions.decision: Modular: clock/ → storage/ → core/ → replication/ → api/
decisions.rationale: Bottom-up dependency chain. Easy to test each layer independently. Clear separation of concerns.

decisions.title: Version Control
decisions.decision: Git with decision tracking in docs/decisions.json
decisions.rationale: Standard version control. centralizes all decisions for viva/review.

decisions.title: Implementation Order (Modules)
decisions.decision: 1. Vector Clock Engine → 2. Storage Adapter → 3. Core Node Service → 4. Replication Manager → 5. REST API → 6. Demo/Tests
decisions.rationale: Bottom-up: lower layers first. Each layer tested before next depends on it.

decisions.title: Innovation Feature #1: Quorum-Based Consistency
decisions.decision: Implement configurable N (replicas), R (read replicas), W (write replicas) parameters
decisions.rationale: Direct from Dynamo paper. Allows tuning consistency vs. availability. Demonstrates production-grade thinking. Academic strength: R + W > N guarantees consistency.
decisions.implementation: Add to Node config: N=3, W=2, R=2. Enforce in replication.sync()

decisions.title: Innovation Feature #2: Conflict Visualization
decisions.decision: Display concurrent versions with their vector clocks and metadata
decisions.rationale: Makes invisible distributed behavior visible. Strong presentation tool. Shows causality detection in action.
decisions.implementation: CLI output + JSON export: { key, versions: [{vc, author, value}, ...] }
decisions.impact: Demonstrable on whiteboard or demo. Clarifies concurrent update detection.

decisions.title: Conflict Resolution Strategies
decisions.decision: Support multiple strategies: multi-version (default), last-write-wins (LWW), custom merge
decisions.rationale: Shows extensibility. LWW common in practice. Custom lets teams implement domain logic.

decisions.title: CLI Interface Layer
decisions.decision: Build interactive CLI using Python click/typer for manual operations and debugging
decisions.rationale: Enables direct node control without UI. Essential for teaching and demo scenarios. Easy to show causality step-by-step.
commands: repliqia put key value [write to node]
decisions.benefit: Professors can follow exact sequence of operations → causality becomes visible

decisions.title: Preact UI Dashboard Layer
decisions.decision: Build real-time web dashboard using Preact + WebSocket for live visualization
decisions.rationale: Interactive visualization of concurrent updates, conflicts, vector clocks. Makes distributed behavior tangible.
decisions.benefit: Visual demo immediately resonates. Can show causality, concurrency, and sync in real-time.

implementation_roadmap.benefit: Real-time interactive demo of distributed system behavior

layer_1_ui.name: Preact UI Dashboard
layer_1_ui.shows: Real-time node topology, conflicts, sync events, vector clocks

layer_2_cli.name: CLI Interface
layer_2_cli.provides: Manual node control, step-by-step causality testing

layer_3_api.name: REST API
layer_3_api.benefit: Distributed system integration + live UI updates

presentation_narrative.title: Repliqia: Dynamo-inspired Vector-Clock-Based Distributed Key-Value Store
presentation_narrative.hook: Built a causality-aware distributed system without central coordination
key_points: Vector clocks detect concurrent edits automatically
Multi-version storage preserves causality on conflicts
Conflict visualization makes invisible system behavior tangible
viva_questions: How do vector clocks differ from timestamps?
Why R + W > N matters for consistency
How does the system handle node failures?

notes: This file tracks ALL architectural decisions and innovations. Multiple versions stored for concurrent updates = Repliqia itself eating its own dog food!
